# go-knifer

> Go utility library organized as public `v*` facade packages backed by implementation packages under `internal/`.

## Project layout

- `v*/`: importable facade packages for users.
- `internal/`: implementation packages shared by facades.
- `bin/`: repository validation scripts.
- `docs/`: project documentation and API stability artifacts.
- `.github/workflows/`: CI and release automation.
- `CLAUDE.md`: AI agent guide with workflow and package catalog.
- `doc.go`: root package with `//go:generate go run ./bin/api_snapshot.go`.

## Package name mapping

| Public facade | Full domain | internal package |
|---|---|---|
| vstr | string/text helpers | internal/str |
| vslice | slice utilities | internal/slice |
| vmap | map utilities | internal/maps |
| vcache | caches (FIFO/LRU/LFU/TTL) | internal/cache |
| vcrypto | cryptography/digests | internal/crypto |
| vhttp | HTTP (stdlib) | internal/httpx |
| vresty | HTTP (Resty) | internal/httpx/resty |
| vjson | JSON | internal/json |
| vconf | configuration | internal/conf |
| vid | UUID/Snowflake/NanoId | internal/id |
| vjwt | JWT sign/verify | internal/jwt |
| vrand | randomness | internal/rand |
| vlog | logging | internal/log |
| verr | error handling (errx) | internal/errx |
| vfile | file/IO | internal/file |
| vurl | URL/URI | internal/url |
| vmask | data masking | internal/mask |
| vcodec | Base64/Hex | internal/codec |
| vhash | non-crypto hashes | internal/hash |
| vzip | archive/compression | internal/zip |
| vset | sets | internal/sets |
| vblf | bloom filter | internal/bloomfilter |
| vbean | struct/map mapping | internal/bean |
| vconv | type conversion | internal/conv |
| vdate | date/time | internal/date |
| vref | reflection | internal/ref |
| vregex | regular expressions | internal/regex |
| vtpl | templates | internal/template |
| vbool | booleans | internal/boolean |
| vnum | numeric helpers | internal/num |
| vcsv | CSV | internal/csvx |
| vimg | images/captchas | internal/imgx |
| vxml | XML | internal/xml |
| vmail | email/SMTP | internal/mail |
| vskt | sockets | internal/socket |
| vnet | IP/port/network | internal/net |
| vdb | database/SQL | internal/db |
| vcron | cron scheduling | internal/cron |
| vjob | job orchestration | internal/job |
| vsem | semaphores | internal/semaphore |
| vdfa | DFA word-tree matching | internal/dfa |
| vpass | password strength | internal/pass |
| vident | identity numbers | internal/identity |
| vform | form/input validation | internal/validator |
| vver | version comparison | internal/version |
| vsys | system information | internal/system |
| vpoi | office documents (Excel) | internal/poi |
| vobj | object helpers | internal/obj |

## Iconography

- `v*` = public facade package, `internal/*` = implementation package.

## Architecture rules

1. Facades are thin — forward to `internal/*`, no business logic.
2. No `v*` imports another `v*` — shared code goes into `internal/*`.
3. Every `v<domain>` has a `doc.go`.
4. `internal/*` never imports `v*`.
5. Production `panic` only in `MustXxx`/`PanicXxx` APIs.
6. Error contract: root `errors.go` defines `ErrCode`, `Error{Code,Message,Cause}`, `CodeCarrier`, `CodeOf`.

## API design

- Prefer `(result, error)` over `panic`.
- IO/network functions take `context.Context` as first parameter.
- Renaming a public symbol = breaking change (SemVer).
- Security-sensitive: vhttp, vresty, vurl, vconf, vzip, vfile, vcrypto, vjwt, vrand, vid, vdb.

## Validation commands

- `make agent-check`: default AI/Agent-safe validation gate; delegates to the fast local gate.
- `make quick-check`: fast local gate with module verification, vet, architecture check, unit tests, API snapshot check, and whitespace diff check.
- `make doctor`: diagnose the local Go/tooling/Git environment without modifying repository files.
- `make worktree-check`: fail when unrelated untracked Go files may pollute tests or commits.
- `make security-sensitive-diff`: detect changes under security-sensitive public facades and their internal implementations.
- `make security-check`: lint and govulncheck vulnerability scan.
- `make agent-security-check`: AI/Agent security validation gate.
- `make full-check COVERAGE_FILE=/tmp/go-knifer-coverage.out`: full local pre-push gate with module verification, vet, architecture check, race/shuffle coverage, coverage gate, API snapshot check, lint, vulnerability scan, and whitespace diff check.
- `make agent-full-check COVERAGE_FILE=/tmp/go-knifer-coverage.out`: full AI/Agent validation gate with coverage, lint, and vulnerability scan.
- `make install-hooks`: enable optional Git hooks that run `make quick-check` before commit and `make full-check COVERAGE_FILE=/tmp/go-knifer-coverage.out` before push.
- `make ai-context-check`: validate machine-readable AI metadata, command side effects, facade inventory, coverage gates, and security-sensitive package references.
- `make ci-test`: GitHub Actions test-job gate.
- `make check`: alias for `make full-check`.
- `make generate`: run repository go:generate directives; ask first because generated files may change.
- `UPDATE_API=1 make api-check`: refresh the API snapshot after intentional public API changes.
- `go generate ./...`: lower-level equivalent of `make generate`.
- `go test -race -shuffle=on -coverprofile=/tmp/go-knifer-coverage.out ./... && bash bin/check_coverage.sh /tmp/go-knifer-coverage.out`: full coverage check.

## Governance notes

- Keep package tests split by source-file responsibility.
- Do not stage, edit, delete, or rely on unrelated untracked Go files unless the user explicitly includes them in scope.
- Keep `ai-context.json` synchronized with Makefile targets, facade inventory, command side effects, command `risk_level`, coverage gates, and security-sensitive package lists.
- Keep `ai-context.json.change_type_policies` synchronized with PR template change types and required Agent validation commands.
- Use `make security-sensitive-diff` to detect whether changed paths require the `security_sensitive` validation policy.
- Keep production code free of forbidden panic patterns checked by `bin/check_arch.sh`.
- Keep total coverage above the threshold defined in `ai-context.json`; `bin/check_coverage.sh` reads AI metadata as the default source of truth.
- API compatibility is CI-enforced via `docs/api/exports.txt` snapshot.
